detail.c125_fix: totalRounds was readonly 8, now mutable tieredValue([6,7,8,8])

detail.note: roundLengths controls sequence length per round (4/5/6 symbols) — primary cognitive-load param, hardcoded, never scales with tier
gridRows derived from totalPairs — scales implicitly
startRadius is intentionally constant (shrink target, not starting zone)

missing_tieredValue: mg_regard.ts — roundLengths: readonly [4,5,6] (3 fixed rounds of 4/5/6 symbols). At tier 0 this gives 4+5+6=15 total targets; at tier 3 the player has less time but the same sequence length — the difficulty asymmetry is incomplete.

bundle_analysis.mg_course_fix_detail: Line 77: this.totalRounds = this.tieredValue([6,7,8,8] as const) confirmed. Field is mutable (no readonly). Comment on line 50 documents the C125 change.
bundle_analysis.bibliotheque_polygonOffset_detail: Lines 176-186 in LairGLBAssets.ts: polygonOffset=true, factor=-2, units=-4 applied in traverse() after applyFlatShading(). Matches mur_pierre/sol_pierre pattern.
bundle_analysis.vite_comment_fix_detail: Line 22-23 now reads: 'Groups registry + base into a shared chunk; individual mg_*.ts files land in their own micro-chunks (dynamically imported via createMinigame).' Comment partially inaccurate — see minigames_chunk_actual_behavior.
bundle_analysis.minigames_chunk_actual_behavior: MinigameRegistry.ts uses STATIC imports of all 14 mg_*.ts files (import { MinigameApaisement } from './mg_apaisement', etc). Because MinigameRegistry.ts is listed in manualChunks.minigames, Rollup pulls all its transitive static dependencies — all 14 mg_*.ts files — into the minigames chunk as well. Result: all 14 files + MinigameBase + MinigameRegistry bundle into minigames-DGjWMdc3.js (119.85 kB gzip 23.56 kB). The vite.config.ts comment 'individual mg_*.ts files land in their own micro-chunks (dynamically imported via createMinigame)' is WRONG — there are no dynamic imports inside MinigameRegistry.ts. The 14 files are co-bundled, not micro-chunked. This is actually the DESIRED behavior (one deferred chunk, cache-friendly) but the comment misrepresents the mechanism.

scores.tieredValue_coverage_note: 13/14 fully tiered. mg_regard.ts roundLengths gap is LOW severity (game is still playable; showTime scaling provides some difficulty gradient).
scores.bundle_strategy_note: Chunk behavior is correct and cache-optimal. -0.5 for vite.config.ts comment that misdescribes the mechanism (static vs dynamic imports). Not a runtime bug.
scores.z_fighting_guards_note: bibliotheque polygonOffset fully confirmed at factor=-2 units=-4. Consistent with mur_pierre/sol_pierre/sol_pierre_tile pattern across all GLB assets.

new_bugs.title: mg_regard.ts: roundLengths hardcoded — sequence length does not scale with difficultyTier
new_bugs.detail: private readonly roundLengths: readonly number[] = [4, 5, 6]. At all tiers the player memorizes sequences of 4, 5, then 6 symbols across 3 rounds. Only showTime scales. Higher tiers compress memorization time but keep the same symbol count — easy tiers could be harder on rounds 2-3, hard tiers could extend to 7+ symbols.
new_bugs.fix: Make roundLengths mutable and set via tieredValue in setup(): this.roundLengths = this.tieredValue([[4,5,6],[4,5,6],[4,6,7],[5,6,8]] as const). Each element is a tuple. Requires tieredValue to support array-of-arrays OR use three separate tieredValue calls for each round slot.

new_bugs.title: vite.config.ts: comment incorrectly states mg_*.ts land in micro-chunks via dynamic import
new_bugs.detail: Comment says 'individual mg_*.ts files land in their own micro-chunks (dynamically imported via createMinigame)' — factually wrong. MinigameRegistry.ts uses static imports, so all 14 files are bundled into the single minigames chunk via static dependency resolution. No micro-chunks are created. The runtime behavior is correct; only the documentation is wrong.

verdict_rationale: C125 fixes verified. 13/14 minigames fully tiered. bibliotheque z-fighting guard confirmed. Bundle chunk behavior is correct (both bugs are LOW severity documentation/completeness issues, no runtime regressions). Score 9.2 is stable vs C61 9.23.
